title: geissois ternata var. ternata
intro: Geissois ternata var. ternata, also known as the Tawa or New Zealand laurel, is a tree species native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive, edible leaves and aromatic wood. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Geissois ternata var. ternata include relief from digestive issues, reduction of fever, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a valuable resource in traditional Maori medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, while the wood is often used in traditional medicine and as a flavoring agent in food.
